name: data-table
description: A server-sorted, server-paginated data table: a real <table> whose sort and page are SERVER facts (sortable headers are real <a href> re-sort links carrying aria-sort, paging via the pagination partial). Rows come in via a Content slot (caller owns the cells). Empty state. Plus the shadcn data-table feature set as server-first slots: a leading checkbox column (select-all + per-row, setting data-state=selected), a toolbar region (search/filter + column-visibility dropdown), and a selected-count footer with a bulk-action slot. Zero JS, zero client sort, CSP-clean. The presentational primitive the kit's admin list composes.
docs: Copies the data-table partial family to your JTE root: lievit/data-table.jte (+ data-table/{row,cell,selection-cell,toolbar,column-visibility,selection-footer}.jte), reached as @template.lievit.data-table(...). It is server-first: sort and page are URL state your controller produces, not client state. Pass `headers` (labels), `sortUrls` (per-column next-sort href; blank = not sortable), `sortStates` ("asc"/"desc"/"" per column), and the `rows` slot (one @template.lievit.data-table.row per item, holding @template.lievit.data-table.cell blocks). Pass `page`/`totalPages`/`pageBaseUrl` for the pager and `rowCount`=0 to render the empty state. SELECTION: add @template.lievit.data-table.selection-cell(header=true,...) as the leading header cell and one per row (a native checkbox; check the row sets data-state=selected), and render the running count with @template.lievit.data-table.selection-footer. FILTER + COLUMNS: put @template.lievit.data-table.toolbar above the table with a search input slot and @template.lievit.data-table.column-visibility (a Columns dropdown of per-column checkbox items) in its `end` slot. Selection / filter / column-visibility are server facts the kit holds; these partials render the structure + the wire hook (l:model / form action), so they work with JS off. The pager composes the sibling `pagination` partial, so copy `lievit/pagination.jte` in too. The lievit-kit admin list (AdminListView/ListRequest) is the stateful engine that drives this primitive via l:click sort/page/select actions; this is the markup it renders. The source is yours: edit freely.
